title: Delhi-NCR, Andhra Pradesh reel under ‘severe’ heatwave — IMD issues heavy rain warning for Bengaluru - Mint
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued another severe heatwave warning as high temperatures continue to scorch northwest and central India. IMD scientist Naresh Kumar told ANI that heatwave conditions are likely to continue for at least another week across large parts of Northwest, Central and East India. “We anticipate that heatwave conditions will persist across parts of Northwest, Central, and most of East India for the next 6 to 7 days,” he said. “We have issued an 'Orange Alert' for Punjab, Haryana, NCR-Delhi region, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Odisha.” The IMD has also issued a ‘Red alert’ for East Uttar Pradesh, East Madhya Pradesh and certain parts of Vidarbha for the next three days. The weather agency issued an “orange” alert for a severe heatwave in Delhi till 27 May. Kumar told ANI that temperatures in Delhi-NCR are hovering above 45°C and similar conditions are expected to continue through the week. According to the IMD’s bulletin, Delhi will experience a mainly clear sky accompanied by gruelling heatwave conditions. Temperatures in Delhi are expected to hover between a scorching 44°C and 46 °C, remaining markedly above normal. The IMD also predicted "Warm Night" conditions at isolated places across Delhi, with minimum temperatures staying well above the seasonal average. The intense heat is not restricted to Delhi. The IMD highlights that: The highest maximum temperature in the country was recently clocked at a blistering 48.2°C in Banda, Uttar Pradesh. Heatwave is expected to intensify over Andhra Pradesh over the next four days, according to the Visakhapatnam Meteorological Department. People have been advised to remain cautious and avoid unnecessary travel between 11 am and 4 pm, when temperatures are expected to remain at their peak, according to an Economic Times (ET) report, citing Jagannath Kumar, official from the regional Met dept. The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) has issued a severe heatwave warning, categorising regional temperature expectations into three primary brackets: A contrasting weather pattern is emerging in eastern and northeastern India, where isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall is projected to cause localised flooding and disruptions. Under the influence of multiple cyclonic circulations and lower-level troughs, major meteorological action is anticipated in the East and Northeast: Bengaluru is expected to experience scattered thunderstorms today, with temperatures hovering around a maximum of 31°C and a minimum of 22°C. The IMD has issued a yellow alert for Bengaluru until Sunday, 24 May, due to unstable pre-monsoon conditions, warning residents to prepare for intense showers, lightning, and gusty winds. “Heavy rain/thundershowers with gusty wind (50-60 kmph) likely to occur at one or two places over Bengaluru (Rural), Bengaluru (Urban),” the regional Met department said in its bulletin. The forecast is also applicable for Kolar, Tumakuru, Chikkaballapura, and Ramanagara districts. At Mint, she regularly writes explainers, quick takes, and visuals‑led stories that are optimized for search and social, while maintaining the publication’s standards for accuracy and clarity. She collaborates closely with editors and the audience team to frame angles that resonate with readers in India and abroad, and to translate complex developments into accessible, high‑impact journalism.

Arshdeep's academic training underpins her interest towards policy and markets. She earned an MA in Economics from Panjab University and holds a Post‑Graduate Diploma in Broadcast Journalism from the India Today Media Institute (ITMI). This blend of economics and broadcast storytelling informs her coverage of public policy, elections, macro themes, and the consumer‑internet zeitgeist.

Arshdeep is based in New Delhi, where she tracks breaking developments and longer‑horizon storylines that shape public discourse.

title: Severe Heatwave Grips Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Temperatures Cross 47 Degrees - The Morning Voice
full_text:
Severe Heatwave Grips Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Temperatures Cross 47 Degrees
Telangana and Andhra Pradesh are reeling under a severe heatwave , with temperatures soaring well above normal for May and crossing the 47°C mark in parts of the region. The scorching conditions have left residents struggling through some of the harshest pre-monsoon heat of the season.
In Telangana, the state capital Hyderabad recorded 100.8°F (38.2°C) on Thursday afternoon, while the day’s peak surged to a dangerous 105.7°F (40.9°C) . Meteorologists have forecast only marginal relief on Friday, 22 May, with highs expected around 101.2°F (38.4°C) and just a 15% chance of rain . The dry and punishing heat is expected to persist, forcing many residents in Hyderabad’s older localities to remain indoors during peak afternoon hours.
Across the border in Andhra Pradesh, conditions have been even more extreme, with Thursday’s maximum temperature touching 110.8°F (43.8°C) , among the highest recorded in the state this year. While Friday may bring a slight dip to 103.7°F (39.8°C) , the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned of a 45% chance of thunderstorms that could trigger sudden and heavy rainfall, particularly along coastal districts such as Visakhapatnam and Vijayawada. Interior regions are expected to continue facing intense heat with minimal cloud cover.
The IMD has issued heatwave warnings across multiple districts in both states, advising vulnerable groups including the elderly, children and outdoor workers to avoid direct sun exposure between 11 AM and 4 PM . Health officials have also reported a rise in heat exhaustion and dehydration cases in government hospitals over the past week.
Looking ahead, Telangana is expected to remain above 99°F through the weekend , while Andhra Pradesh may see more volatile weather conditions with rising rain chances of 40–45 percent , indicating possible early signs of monsoon activity offshore.
Experts have cautioned that until the southwest monsoon arrives, likely in early June, extreme caution is needed outdoors. “This is peak heat stress season,” a senior IMD official said, urging residents to stay hydrated, wear light clothing and follow all advisories.

title: Andhra Pradesh heatwave: Piduguralla 48.1°C, 325 suspected heatstrokes statewide - NewsBytes
full_text:
Andhra Pradesh heatwave: Piduguralla 48.1°C, 325 suspected heatstrokes statewide
Andhra Pradesh is dealing with an intense heat wave, with some places like Piduguralla in Palnadu district hitting 48.1 degrees Celsius.
From March to mid-May, there have been 325 suspected heatstroke cases, 96 just this month.
Visakhapatnam tops the list for cases, followed by Kakinada and Vizianagaram.
Andhra Pradesh warns 45 to 47°C
The heat wave isn't letting up anytime soon. APSDMA says temperatures are likely to remain between 45 degrees Celsius and 47 degrees Celsius in several districts until Monday.
200 mandals across the state are seeing extreme conditions. The government has stocked up on ORS packets (more than 10 million) and is sending out district-level alerts to help people stay safe.
Thankfully, no heatwave deaths have been reported so far this year, showing better preparedness compared with last year's numbers.
